findings.description: schema.sql references undefined enum types: 'analysisstatus_themecategory' for category column (should reference existing 'themecategory' enum) and 'themeTrend' for trend column (no CREATE TYPE block). Schema will fail to compile in isolation.
findings.resolution: Add DO $$ BEGIN CREATE TYPE ... EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object blocks for themecategory and themetrend enums, or document that these are created by existing migrations and this schema is additive only.

findings.description: No slug collision handling specified. If two themes produce the same slug (e.g., 'RAG Architecture' and 'rag-architecture'), the UNIQUE constraint will throw an unhandled error. No retry-with-suffix or deduplication strategy defined.
findings.resolution: Add a requirement: 'WHEN slug generation produces a duplicate, the system SHALL append a numeric suffix (-2, -3) to ensure uniqueness.'

findings.description: Index records stored as regular Topic records (slug prefix '_') will appear in normal topic listings unless explicitly filtered. GET /api/v1/kb/topics without a filter returns index records mixed with real topics. No filtering logic is specified in spec, design, or OpenAPI contract.
findings.resolution: Add a WHERE clause excluding '_' prefixed slugs from default topic list queries, or add an 'is_index' boolean column. Update spec scenario for list topics to state 'index records SHALL NOT appear in default listings.'

findings.description: OpenAPI TopicDetail schema is missing 'merged_into_id' field which is defined in spec.md (Requirement 1, lifecycle transitions) and schema.sql. Clients cannot see merge targets.
findings.resolution: Add 'merged_into_id' (integer, nullable) to TopicDetail schema in v1.yaml.

findings.description: TopicStatus lifecycle has 5 states (DRAFT, ACTIVE, STALE, ARCHIVED, MERGED) but no valid transition rules specified. Can a MERGED topic transition back to ACTIVE? Can ARCHIVED unarchive? No state machine diagram or transition requirements.
findings.resolution: Add a requirement specifying valid transitions: DRAFT→ACTIVE, ACTIVE→STALE, ACTIVE→ARCHIVED, ACTIVE→MERGED, STALE→ACTIVE (on new evidence), STALE→ARCHIVED. MERGED and ARCHIVED are terminal unless explicitly reversed.

findings.description: No error handling or rollback strategy for compilation failures. If LLM fails mid-article or GraphitiClient fails during relationship sync, the system has no specified behavior. CompilationResult in OpenAPI lacks error fields.
findings.resolution: Add error handling requirement: compilation SHALL be atomic per-topic (topic either fully updates or rolls back). Add 'errors' array to CompilationResult schema showing failed topics with reasons.

findings.description: OpenAPI GET /topics lacks sort_by parameter but spec says 'default sort SHALL be by relevance_score descending'. No way for API consumers to sort by name, date, or mention_count.
findings.resolution: Add 'sort_by' query parameter with options: relevance_score (default), name, mention_count, last_compiled_at, created_at. Add 'sort_order' parameter (asc/desc).

findings.description: No authentication/authorization specified for KB endpoints. Existing API uses X-Admin-Key header. Spec and OpenAPI don't mention auth requirements for any KB endpoint. Write operations (POST, PATCH, DELETE) especially need protection.
findings.resolution: Add requirement: all KB write endpoints SHALL require X-Admin-Key authentication. Read endpoints (GET) SHALL follow the same auth policy as existing content/digest endpoints.

findings.description: Concurrent compilation runs can race on index records (_index, _by_category). Two parallel compiles both trying to upsert slug='_index' will hit UNIQUE constraint. No UPSERT or locking strategy specified.
findings.resolution: Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (slug) DO UPDATE for index records. Add advisory lock or single-writer pattern for compilation.

findings.description: Parent-child topic hierarchy has no depth limit. Chains of A→B→C→...→Z are possible. Merge chains (A merged_into B, B merged_into C) also have no cycle detection.
findings.resolution: Add constraint: parent_topic_id chains SHALL NOT exceed depth 5. merged_into_id SHALL NOT create chains (merged topic must point to a non-merged topic).

findings.description: No logging, monitoring, or alerting requirements specified for any KB service. Compilation is a critical pipeline step that adds 30-60s — failures need visibility.
findings.resolution: Add requirement: KB compilation SHALL emit structured log events for start/complete/error. Add compilation_errors count to health endpoint. Consider notification event emission on compilation failure.

findings.description: OpenAPI lists 7 endpoint groups but proposal says '6 MCP tools' while spec lists 6 MCP tools including 'update_topic'. The MCP tool count is consistent but the spec's update_topic tool and the API's PATCH endpoint have different semantics — MCP updates 'a section' while API updates 'topic fields'.
findings.resolution: Clarify MCP update_topic: does it update article_md sections (content-level) or topic fields (metadata-level)? Align with PATCH endpoint semantics.

findings.description: schema.sql missing composite index on (status, last_compiled_at) needed for stale topic detection query in health checks. Also missing (created_at DESC) for recency queries.
findings.resolution: Add indexes: ix_topics_status_compiled (status, last_compiled_at) and ix_topics_created_at (created_at DESC).

findings.description: No backward compatibility strategy if ThemeData Pydantic model gains new fields in the future. Topic model as 'full superset' means any ThemeData change requires an Alembic migration.
findings.resolution: Document this as a known maintenance cost in design.md D2 trade-offs. Consider a JSONB 'extra_fields' column for future-proofing.

findings.description: Work package wp-integration depends on wp-cli, wp-api, wp-agent, wp-pipeline but NOT wp-contracts. Integration tests may need contract schemas for API validation.
findings.resolution: Add wp-contracts as a dependency of wp-integration.
